take place.explanation_en: to happen, especially in a particular place or situation
take place.example_sentence: The meeting will take place in the main hall.
take place.original_sentence: Every day, it seems, this takes place in the fast-moving world of business.

fast-moving.explanation_zh: 快速变化的，发展迅速的
fast-moving.example_sentence: She works in a fast-moving industry where things change daily.
fast-moving.original_sentence: Every day, it seems, this takes place in the fast-moving world of business.

call in.explanation_en: to ask someone to come to a room or office, usually to talk
call in.example_sentence: The manager called in the team for a quick meeting.
call in.original_sentence: The boss calls in an employee and explains that a special task must be accomplished.

accomplished.example_sentence: She felt proud to have accomplished the difficult task.
accomplished.original_sentence: The boss calls in an employee and explains that a special task must be accomplished.

handle.explanation_en: to deal with something or be responsible for it
handle.example_sentence: Can you handle this project on your own?
handle.original_sentence: Then he says, I know you got a lot of work to do, but can you handle this?

awfully.example_sentence: I'm awfully tired after the long trip.
awfully.original_sentence: Too often the employee replies, I'm awfully sorry, but I'm all loaded down now.

loaded down.explanation_en: having too much work to do or too many things to carry
loaded down.explanation_zh: 忙得不可开交，负担过重
loaded down.example_sentence: I can't go out tonight, I'm too loaded down with work.
loaded down.original_sentence: Too often the employee replies, I'm awfully sorry, but I'm all loaded down now.

take on.explanation_en: to accept a new task or responsibility
take on.example_sentence: She decided to take on the extra project even though she was busy.
take on.original_sentence: I wish I could take it on, but I'm just too busy.

under the circumstances.explanation_en: considering the situation; because of the situation
under the circumstances.example_sentence: Under the circumstances, I think we should cancel the trip.
under the circumstances.original_sentence: Under the circumstances, the boss doesn't hold it against the employee because it is extra duty, so to speak.

hold it against.explanation_en: to feel angry or upset with someone because of something they did
hold it against.explanation_zh: 因某事对某人怀恨在心
hold it against.example_sentence: I made a mistake, but I hope you won't hold it against me.
hold it against.original_sentence: Under the circumstances, the boss doesn't hold it against the employee because it is extra duty, so to speak.

so to speak.explanation_en: used to show that you are using a special expression or an unusual way of saying something
so to speak.example_sentence: He is, so to speak, the heart of the team.
so to speak.original_sentence: Under the circumstances, the boss doesn't hold it against the employee because it is extra duty, so to speak.

forge ahead.explanation_en: to move forward steadily and make progress, especially after difficulties
forge ahead.example_sentence: Despite the challenges, the team forged ahead with the project.
forge ahead.original_sentence: And this employee is the fellow who will forge ahead.

output.explanation_en: the amount of work or goods produced by a person or machine
output.example_sentence: The factory has increased its output by 20% this year.
output.original_sentence: In business, in the home, in the community, the success combination is do what you do better, improve the quality of your output, and do more of what you do, increase the quantity of your output.

stand out.explanation_en: to be easily seen or noticed because you are different or better
stand out.example_sentence: Her excellent presentation made her stand out from the other candidates.
stand out.original_sentence: Accepting greater responsibility on the job makes you stand out and shows that you're more valuable.

civic.explanation_en: relating to a city or town, or the people who live there
civic.example_sentence: It's important to take part in civic activities in your community.
civic.original_sentence: When your neighbors ask you to represent them on a civic matter, accept.

shortcut.explanation_en: a quicker or easier way of doing something
shortcut.example_sentence: There are no shortcuts to learning a language well.
shortcut.original_sentence: Some of these answers may be better planning and organization of your present work, or taking intelligent shortcuts in your routine activities, or possibly dropping non-essential activities altogether.

non-essential.example_sentence: During the meeting, please avoid non-essential talk.
non-essential.original_sentence: Some of these answers may be better planning and organization of your present work, or taking intelligent shortcuts in your routine activities, or possibly dropping non-essential activities altogether.

count on.explanation_en: to depend on someone or something, to trust that they will do what you expect
count on.example_sentence: You can always count on him to help when you're in trouble.
count on.original_sentence: I have learned in dozens of instances that I can count on a busy man to deliver, but I have often been disappointed in working with people who have all the time in the world.

deliver.explanation_en: to do what you promised or are expected to do
deliver.example_sentence: If we promise to finish by Friday, we must deliver.
deliver.original_sentence: I have learned in dozens of instances that I can count on a busy man to deliver.

progressive.explanation_en: favoring new ideas and modern methods, always trying to improve
progressive.example_sentence: The company has a progressive policy on working from home.
progressive.original_sentence: Progressive business management constantly asks, what can we do to expand output?
